pages.raw_text:
The members of the Northwest
Literary club will entertain their
husbands and few friends at
their annual reception this evening
at the home of Mrs. e. Tiff, 1926
musical and literary program and
dainty refreshments will be served.
About 80 guests have been included
on the invitation list.


81 4 = 4


Mrs. J. II. Mower was hostess
this afternoon when she enter
tanned the members of Chapter e
of the =. +. o. club at her home,
on Nora avenue. Mrs. Mosher was
chairman of the entertainment
committee and most entertaining
numbers was given during the aft
ernoon. At late hour delightful
refreshments were served


= q 4


The domestic science department
of the Woman's club held their reg
ular meeting this afternoon at the
North Central high school.


= = q


Mrs. Henrietta Clark entertained
the members of the East End Book
club this afternoon at the home of
her daughter, Mrs. II. e. Schlegel,
E20ll Sprague avenue. The after
noon was spent with regular bust
ness meeting of the club. followed
by program. which was arranged
by Mrs. Iva Gaisford and Mrs. Myr.
tie Gardner.


The regular rehearsal of the
Symphony orchestra was held this
morning.


In compliment to Miss Gladys
Labaree who leaves shortly for
Europe. Miss Lenore Townsend was
hostess this afternoon when she
entertained at charming luncheon
and cards. A beautiful bouquet of
violets formed the center piece at
the luncheon, where covers were
placed for 20 guests. Following the
luncheon the social hours were
spent at bridge.


= q 4


There was a regular business
meeting this afternoon of the
Equal Suffragist club at the assem-
bly, room of the chamber of com
merce. 1t was an annual meeting
for the election of officers. II. l..
Hughes, editor of the Labor World,
addressed the meeting.


q = =


The North Side chapter of the
All Saints cathedral met this after
noon at o'clock with Mrs. Robert
Simpson ut her home ON Baldwin
avenue.
